page.content: In the 3rd Quarter of 2023, Merck & Co announced several significant licensing and partnership deals, detailed in the Licensing Deals Overview table. The Oncology Collaboration deal, announced on 2023-09-15, involved an upfront payment of 20M SGD with a royalty rate of 5%, contributing to a total potential value of 100M SGD. The Vaccine Development deal, announced on 2023-08-01, featured an upfront payment of 30M SGD, a royalty rate of 7%, and a total potential value of 150M SGD.

lines.text: In the 3rd Quarter of 2023, Merck & Co announced several significant licensing and partnership deals, detailed in the Licensing
Deals Overview table. The Oncology Collaboration deal, announced on 2023-09-15, involved an upfront payment of 20M SGD
with a royalty rate of 5%, contributing to a total potential value of 100M SGD. The Vaccine Development deal, announced on
2023-08-01, featured an upfront payment of 30M SGD, a royalty rate of 7%, and a total potential value of 150M SGD.

page.content: The Cardiovascular Research deal, dated 2023-07-10, included an upfront payment of 15M SGD, a 6% royalty rate, and a total potential value of 80M SGD.

lines.text: The Cardiovascular Research deal, dated 2023-07-10, included an upfront payment of 15M SGD, a 6% royalty rate, and a total

page.content: The Rare Disease Therapy deal, announced on 2023-06-05, had an upfront payment of 10M SGD, a 4% royalty, and a total potential value of 50M SGD. The Neurology Partnership deal, announced on 2023-05-20, involved an upfront payment of 25M SGD, an 8% royalty rate, and a total potential value of 120M SGD. The Immunotherapy Collaboration deal, dated 2023-04-15, featured a 35M SGD upfront payment, a 9% royalty rate, and a total potential value of 200M SGD. The total deals for the quarter amounted to 135M SGD in upfront payments, a 39% royalty rate, and a total potential value of 700M SGD. Concurrently, in the Biosimilar Product table, Biosimilar A sold 15,000 units with a growth rate of 25%, generating revenue of $3.2M in a market size that reflects its position. Biosimilar B recorded 12,000 units sold, with a growth rate of 20%, resulting in $2.5M in revenue. Biosimilar C sold 8,000 units, achieving a 15% growth rate and generating $1.8M in revenue.
